title: Generate a balance / minimum-overlap CBC design
summary: Construct a multi-version Choice-Based Conjoint design by minimising one-hot pair-count and marginal-count SSE at task and version levels. Heuristic but very fast; produces highly balanced designs without any prior on respondent utilities.
description: Searches the space of (V × T × C × K) integer level matrices using simulated annealing. The objective sums two SSE terms: pair-count balance (X^T X for each task should match its expected value under independent uniform sampling, scaled by V*T) and marginal-count balance (sum_c X[c,:] for each task). Constraints supported: pairwise restrictions, parent-child gating, fixed cells, and per-attribute level weights. Multi-start parallel search via `n_starts` + `threads` is the most effective lever for solution quality.
usage:
[["design-cbc::design-bo"]]
run_dir = "<path>"
run_name = "<id>"
# spec.* = the CBC structural inputs (versions, tasks_per_version, concepts_per_task, attributes, ...)
# optional: seed, n_steps, n_starts, threads, weights.*

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Workspace directory; primary output goes to {run_dir}/{run_name}_design.csv.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Run identifier; used as a filename prefix and to namespace ocinfo files.

arguments.name: spec
arguments.description: CBC spec input. Required fields: versions, tasks_per_version, concepts_per_task, attributes (each with name + max_level). Optional: pairwise_restrictions, gating_rules, fixed_cells, fixed_attributes, level_weights.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: BO objective weights: w_total (default 1.0), w_ver (1.0), w_task_pairs (1.0), w_task_margins (0.2).

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: Base seed; per-start seeds are derived as base + i. Omit for non-deterministic.

arguments.name: cooling_rate
arguments.description: Per-step multiplicative cooling factor; T = T0 * cooling_rate^step.

arguments.name: keep_best
arguments.description: Snapshot the best-seen design and restore it at the end of each start.

arguments.name: n_starts
arguments.description: Number of independent SA chains; the lowest-scoring design wins.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Worker threads for parallel restarts (only meaningful when n_starts > 1).

arguments.name: report_every
arguments.description: Reserved for future progress envelopes (currently informational).

value:
On success, `run_dir` contains:
- `{run_name}_design.csv` — wide design (Version, Task, Concept, <attribute columns>).
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/design_long.csv` — long design (one row per cell).
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/summary_overview.csv` — final score, iterations, accepted moves.
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/summary_main_counts.csv` — per-attribute level counts.
- `ocinfo/{run_name}_design/design.json`, `summary.json`, `spec.input.json` — machine-readable outputs.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, one `event.artifact.created` per output, and a final `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: BO is utility-agnostic: it does not require a prior on respondent betas. Use it when no pilot data exists or when balance/coverage is the explicit goal.
Forbidden combinations are enforced as hard feasibility constraints — any proposal that would create one is rejected before scoring.
Gating rules zero out child attributes when the parent is not in an enabling level. Initial random sampling and proposals respect this.
Multi-start (`n_starts` 4–8 with matched `threads`) is more effective than longer single chains.

see_also.description: D-efficient variant; useful when prior beliefs about respondent utilities exist.

artifacts.title: CBC design — wide format
artifacts.description: The primary design file for survey programming. One row per (version, task, concept) with integer attribute level codes. Supply this file to your survey platform and to assess-design for quality verification.

fields.name: Version
fields.description: Design version number. Each respondent is randomly assigned one version.

fields.name: Concept
fields.description: Concept (product alternative) position within the task.

fields.name: (attribute columns)
fields.description: Integer level codes for each attribute — one column per attribute in the spec.

artifacts.title: Design — long format
artifacts.description: Alternative representation with one row per (version, task, concept, attribute). Useful for programmatic inspection and quality checks.

artifacts.title: Design quality summary
artifacts.description: Final balance/overlap score, iteration count, and accepted-move statistics. Lower scores indicate better-balanced designs.

artifacts.title: Attribute level frequency counts
artifacts.description: How often each level of each attribute appears across the design. Roughly equal counts per level indicate good balance.

details:
The BO objective jointly minimises two SSE terms: pair-count balance (how evenly each attribute-level pair co-appears across tasks) and marginal-count balance (how evenly each attribute level appears across tasks and versions). Neither term requires a prior on respondent utilities, making BO the right choice when no pilot data exists.

The search engine is simulated annealing (SA). Each step proposes a small random perturbation — either swapping one attribute-level assignment within a task or replacing a full task-attribute vector — and accepts or rejects the change based on the change in objective and the current temperature. Temperature decays multiplicatively by `cooling_rate` each step. Running multiple independent SA chains (`n_starts` > 1, with matching `threads`) and keeping the best result is more effective than a single long chain, because the objective surface can have many local minima.

Forbidden combinations (pairwise restrictions, gating rules) are enforced as hard constraints — any proposal that would create a forbidden combination is rejected before scoring. Fixed cells hold specific attribute-level assignments constant throughout the search.

examples.title: Generate a 4-version, 8-task, 3-concept CBC design
examples.body:
[["design-cbc::design-bo"]]
run_dir = "./designs"
run_name = "bo1"
seed = 42
n_starts = 4
threads = 4

[spec]
versions = 4
tasks_per_version = 8
concepts_per_task = 3

[[spec.attributes]]
name = "Brand"
max_level = 4

[[spec.attributes]]
name = "Price"
max_level = 3

[[spec.attributes]]
name = "Feature"
max_level = 3
